hero_subhead:
AI's taking careers. Climate's taking Miami. Fascism's taking attendance.

We wrote the Green New Deal. Now we're writing the Mission for America — the plan the next president will need on day one. We're not waiting.

Three existential crises. Interconnected. Accelerating. Each makes the others worse. There's no way to solve one without solving the others.

That's not a problem. That's the answer.
end_teaser: "Things are not fine. Let's start there."
end_headline: Things are not fine. Let's start there.
end_body:
Three crises. Each accelerating. Each making each other worse. The people who should be fixing it are torn between denial and nihilism. They offer only to slow the descent into chaos.

We believe a different future is possible.
begin_body:
The last time the apocalypse came knocking, we chose to act. Not manage. Not adapt. Mobilize. Built a new economy overnight. Put millions of people to work in really good jobs. Defeated fascism. Not because it was easy. Because that's what it took to save the world.

Time to save the world again. Every country must do everything it can. We can mobilize to build a clean economy for the U.S. and to build the industries to supply the global transition.

Things can only get worse until January 20, 2029. But then we have our chance. Imagine landing in that moment WITHOUT a plan?

We're making the plan.
pivot_line: The end of the world and the beginning of utopia are the same moment. Which one you get depends on what you decide to do next.
mission_body: The full plan. 20+ chapters. Every sector.
done_before_head: we've done this before. and we're doing it again.
done_before_body:
Last time the apocalypse came knocking, we built a new economy. Defeated fascism. Created the middle class. Whole new industries overnight. Millions of unemployed trained into high-paying jobs.

We would have stuck with managed decline. We chose to build a new world.

We wrote the Green New Deal. Now we're writing the Mission for America. A new president takes office in 2029. That's America's next chance. It could be our last.
thinktank_head: a think tank for the emergency.
thinktank_body:
Most think tanks write policy designed to be "realistic." Small ideas that can survive a divided Congress. That's important work.

Our job is different.

New Consensus develops solutions that match the scale of our problems. We wrote the Green New Deal. Now we're writing the Mission for America. Because "realistic" got us here. And here is three converging catastrophes with no serious plan in sight.
join_body: If you're ready to help build the plan, start with the Mission for America and get in touch.
footer_tagline: An economic policy think tank for people who think "realistic" policy got us here.
